sql-analysis-reporting.title: SQL Analysis & Reporting
sql-analysis-reporting.description: Build trustworthy single-table reports from readable detail rows through executive scorecards.
sql-analysis-reporting.moreComingSoon: More SQL courses will extend these skills into multi-table analysis.

sql-analysis-reporting-module-0-report-f.title: Readable Report Foundations
sql-analysis-reporting-module-0-report-f.description: Shape clear row-level reports with aliases, unique values, calculated columns, and consistent rounding.

outcomes.0: Rename result columns without changing the database schema.
outcomes.1: Use DISTINCT to inspect available reporting categories.
outcomes.2: Build row-level calculations from existing numeric columns.
outcomes.3: Round presentation values while preserving the source data.

why.0: Move from basic retrieval to reports another person can read and trust.
why.1: Build a completed-order value report with readable headings, rounded gross sales, and stable sorting.

sql-analysis-reporting-module-1-null-saf.title: Null-Safe Calculations and Business Labels
sql-analysis-reporting-module-1-null-saf.description: Control how missing values affect calculations, then translate row-level results into useful review labels.

outcomes.0: Predict how NULL changes arithmetic results.
outcomes.1: Use COALESCE only when a documented fallback is appropriate.
outcomes.2: Calculate gross, discount, and net values consistently.
outcomes.3: Use CASE to label rows without hiding missing data.

why.0: Make reporting rules explicit whenever source data may be missing.
why.1: Build a discount audit that retains raw values, calculates gross and net sales, and flags records needing review.

sql-analysis-reporting-module-2-aggregat.title: Aggregate Functions and KPI Snapshots
sql-analysis-reporting-module-2-aggregat.description: Condense many rows into trustworthy counts, totals, averages, minimums, and maximums.

outcomes.1: Sum stored columns and calculated expressions.
outcomes.2: Explain how AVG handles missing values.
outcomes.3: Use MIN and MAX to describe a metric range.

why.0: Teach learners to define one-row KPIs before introducing grouped scorecards.
why.1: Build a one-row completed-sales snapshot that preserves every metric added in earlier steps.

sql-analysis-reporting-module-3-grouped-.title: Grouped Summaries and Ranked Scorecards
sql-analysis-reporting-module-3-grouped-.description: Create one summary row per group, preserve the intended report grain, and rank results predictably.

outcomes.0: Group an aggregate report by one dimension.
outcomes.1: Define the grain created by multiple grouping columns.
outcomes.2: Sort aggregate results with deterministic tie-breakers.
outcomes.3: Remove columns that accidentally split a report into extra groups.

why.0: Move from one overall KPI row to comparable regional and category scorecards.
why.1: Build a completed-order region-category scorecard with counts, sales KPIs, ratings, and deterministic ranking.

sql-analysis-reporting-module-4-having-a.title: HAVING and Conditional Reporting
sql-analysis-reporting-module-4-having-a.description: Filter source rows and grouped results at the correct stage, then calculate conditional KPIs and exception labels.

outcomes.0: Use WHERE to control which rows enter an aggregate.
outcomes.1: Use HAVING to keep or remove completed groups.
outcomes.2: Calculate conditional counts and sums with CASE inside aggregates.
outcomes.3: Assign performance labels from aggregate results.

why.0: Turn full scorecards into focused reports that support management action.
why.1: Build a region-category exception report that identifies completion and rating risks without losing prior metrics.

sql-analysis-reporting-module-5-final-ca.title: Final Capstone: Executive Sales Performance Report
sql-analysis-reporting-module-5-final-ca.description: Plan, audit, build, validate, and interpret one complete executive report using the full single-table toolkit.

outcomes.0: Translate a stakeholder brief into a precise report contract.
outcomes.1: Audit source coverage and missing values before implementation.
outcomes.3: Build one cumulative eight-step executive scorecard.

why.0: Integrate the course into a portfolio-quality report with a clear audience, grain, metric contract, and priority order.
why.1: Complete one eight-step executive sales performance report; the four preparation topics remain ordinary lessons.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-0-readabl.title: Readable Report Output
sql-analysis-reporting-section-0-readabl.description: Turn database-oriented columns into clear report output.

bullets.0: Rename result columns so a report communicates business meaning without changing the stored table.
bullets.1: Inspect the unique values and combinations available before defining a report filter or grouping rule.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-0-calcula.title: Calculated Report Columns
sql-analysis-reporting-section-0-calcula.description: Create and format useful row-level metrics.

bullets.0: Create row-level metrics from numeric columns while keeping the original source data unchanged.
bullets.1: Format calculated numbers for a readable report without changing the precision stored in the source table.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-0-module-.title: Module Project
sql-analysis-reporting-section-0-module-.description: Build one clean detail report without discarding earlier work.

bullets.0: Build one readable completed-order detail report, adding aliases, a gross-sales calculation, rounding, and stable sorting without discarding earlier columns.
bullets.1: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.2: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-1-null-sa.title: Null-Safe Calculations
sql-analysis-reporting-section-1-null-sa.description: Understand missing values before deciding how a report should treat them.

bullets.0: Recognize when a missing source value makes a calculated result unknown instead of zero.
bullets.1: Apply an explicit fallback when the reporting rule defines how a missing value should be treated.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-1-busines.title: Business Calculations and Labels
sql-analysis-reporting-section-1-busines.description: Turn percentage rules into readable financial output.

bullets.0: Translate a percentage stored in the table into gross, discount, and net monetary values.
bullets.1: Turn row-level values into clear categories while handling missing data before numeric thresholds.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-1-module-.title: Module Project
sql-analysis-reporting-section-1-module-.description: Create a review-ready discount audit in cumulative steps.

bullets.0: Build a row-level audit that preserves raw discount data, applies an explicit fallback for calculations, and identifies records requiring review.
bullets.1: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.2: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-2-counts-.title: Counts and Totals
sql-analysis-reporting-section-2-counts-.description: Measure activity volume and monetary value.

bullets.0: Use COUNT deliberately to distinguish total records from records with a known value.
bullets.1: Add stored values or calculated row expressions across the full query scope.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-2-average.title: Averages and Extremes
sql-analysis-reporting-section-2-average.description: Describe typical values and useful boundaries.

bullets.0: Calculate an average and state clearly whether missing values are excluded or replaced.
bullets.1: Use MIN and MAX to describe the lower and upper boundaries of a numeric metric.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-2-module-.title: Module Project
sql-analysis-reporting-section-2-module-.description: Combine completed-order KPIs into one reliable snapshot.

bullets.0: Build one cumulative summary row for completed orders, preserving every previously added KPI.
bullets.1: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.2: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-3-groupin.title: Grouping Basics
sql-analysis-reporting-section-3-groupin.description: Define what one row represents in a grouped report.

bullets.0: Return one aggregate summary row for each value of a chosen business dimension.
bullets.1: Define a more detailed report grain by grouping on a deliberate combination of dimensions.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-3-ranking.title: Ranking and Grouping Quality
sql-analysis-reporting-section-3-ranking.description: Order grouped results and prevent accidental grain changes.

bullets.0: Rank grouped metrics and add deterministic tie-breakers so repeated runs produce a predictable order.
bullets.1: Protect report grain by removing detail columns that split or confuse grouped results.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-3-module-.title: Module Project
sql-analysis-reporting-section-3-module-.description: Build one region-category scorecard from a stable base query.

bullets.0: Build one completed-order scorecard at region-category grain and retain every metric as the report grows.
bullets.1: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.2: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-4-group-f.title: Filtering Before and After Grouping
sql-analysis-reporting-section-4-group-f.description: Place conditions where they match the report logic.

bullets.0: Filter source rows before aggregation so every metric describes the intended population.
bullets.1: Keep only completed groups whose aggregate results meet a meaningful threshold.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-4-conditi.title: Conditional KPIs and Labels
sql-analysis-reporting-section-4-conditi.description: Calculate several outcomes from the same grouped rows.

bullets.0: Calculate condition-specific counts and monetary totals without removing the other rows from each group.
bullets.1: Use CASE on aggregate expressions to turn grouped metrics into concise management labels.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-4-module-.title: Module Project
sql-analysis-reporting-section-4-module-.description: Build a focused exception report with calibrated rules.

bullets.0: Build one region-category exception report that compares total and completed activity, calculates risk metrics, and keeps only groups needing attention.
bullets.1: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.2: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-5-capston.title: Capstone Planning
sql-analysis-reporting-section-5-capston.description: Turn a business request into a precise SQL report contract.

bullets.0: Convert a stakeholder request into dimensions, metrics, filters, labels, and ordering rules before writing SQL.
bullets.1: Map each requested output column to its source fields, SQL expression, missing-value rule, and display format.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-5-capston.title: Capstone Data Readiness
sql-analysis-reporting-section-5-capston.description: Audit the table and verify the planned grouping before the final build.

bullets.0: Check status coverage, date coverage, and missing-value exposure before building the capstone metrics.
bullets.1: Prototype the final group key and stable ranking before adding every capstone metric.
bullets.2: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.3: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.

sql-analysis-reporting-section-5-final-c.title: Final Capstone Project
sql-analysis-reporting-section-5-final-c.description: Build the final report through eight cumulative query snapshots.

bullets.0: Build one January 2026 region-category report that combines volume, completion, sales, ratings, data quality, filtering, labels, and deterministic ranking.
bullets.1: Run each query and inspect the results before continuing.
bullets.2: Keep the requested report grain and output order consistent.
